frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Kamla: "Woh jo Sharma ji ki bahu hai, uski toh abhi beti choti hai na? Par woh toh subah se shaam tak office mein rehti hai. Bachhe ko dekhne wala kaun hai? Aise mein toh maa ko thoda ghar par hi rukna chahiye, bachpan wapas thodi aata hai. Career toh baad mein bhi ho jayega, par bachhon ko maa ka pyaar aur dhyan sabse zyada chahiye."
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Kamla, a 58-year-old woman, expresses concern about a young working mother, implying that the mother should prioritize staying home with her child over her career. This encodes the surface manifestation of expecting the mother to sacrifice her career for the family, framed as genuine concern for the child's well-being and using the 'career sacrifice' linguistic cue.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 58. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your son Rohit who is 30 is looking for a bride. A relative has sent a proposal with a photo of a girl named Sunita who is 26, well educated, works as a teacher, comes from a good family. You are looking at the photo with your husband in the bedroom. Sunita is dark skinned. Everything else about her is exactly what you were looking for. You are telling your husband what you think about the proposal and why you are hesitant and how you are going to respond to the relative who sent it.
